items.title: UN Experts Call For Malaysia To Strengthen The Social Work Profession — Social Workers Alliance Malaysia
items.summary: SWAM calls on the government to honour the UNCRC by ensuring the upcoming Social Work Profession Bill includes the public sector. The post UN Experts Call For Malaysia To Strengthen The Social Work Profession — Social Workers Alliance Malaysia appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Houseman Mental Health: Why We Must Look Beyond The Ward — Dr Lim Poh Khuen
items.summary: Hospitals must ensure accessible mental health services, mentorship, and safe channels for house officers to seek help without fear of judgement. The post Houseman Mental Health: Why We Must Look Beyond The Ward — Dr Lim Poh Khuen appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Iran War May Raise Food Prices In Malaysia, Warns MCA
items.summary: MCA has warned that the Iran conflict may lead to rising food prices in Malaysia that's dependent on food imports. Shifting the responsibility for resolving food supply issues onto ordinary citizens won't ease the current pressure of rising food prices. The post Iran War May Raise Food Prices In Malaysia, Warns MCA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia Doesn’t Need Your EPF Savings To Fix Hospital Bills — Dr Pearl Leong
items.summary: Allowing Malaysians to use EPF to buy medical insurance means that we're using our retirement savings to keep funding large hospital bills in private hospitals. GLCs and GLICs that own major hospital groups should control hospital charges instead. The post Malaysia Doesn’t Need Your EPF Savings To Fix Hospital Bills — Dr Pearl Leong appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Intervention Today, Stability Tomorrow — Jasmin Jalil
items.summary: Malaysia’s progress against HIV reflects strong national leadership by the Ministry  of Health, supported by civil society and responsible corporate partners working  together to ensure that no one is left behind. The post Intervention Today, Stability Tomorrow — Jasmin Jalil appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Prudential Paid Out RM1m In Medical Claims To A Policyholder In 2025
items.summary: Prudential paid out RM1.04 million in claims in one policy year to a policyholder undergoing breast cancer treatment in 2025, saying medical plans with RM50k-RM100k annual limits were common in 2013 but are no longer adequate for today’s health care needs. The post Prudential Paid Out RM1m In Medical Claims To A Policyholder In 2025 appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: A ‘Just Culture’ Must Be The Foundation Of Our Health Care System — Dr Musa Mohd Nordin & Prof Azizi Haji Omar
items.summary: The MOH needs a cultural reset, not just a strategic one. This means moving beyond task forces and press releases to embrace a "Just Culture" in every hospital, clinic, and department. A Just Culture isn't about being soft, but being fair and systematic. The post A ‘Just Culture’ Must Be The Foundation Of Our Health Care System — Dr Musa Mohd Nordin & Prof Azizi Haji Omar appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Back To Basics: Reclaiming The Soul Of Medicine — Medical Officer
items.summary: Family medicine, community health, and preventive medicine are seen by some as less prestigious, complex, or important. But who catches disease early? Who manages chronic illness over decades? Primary care isn't lesser medicine; it's foundational medicine. The post Back To Basics: Reclaiming The Soul Of Medicine — Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Seberang Jaya Hospital Leads The Way In The School In Hospital Initiative
items.summary: No child should have to choose between treatment and learning. The School in Hospital initiative helps ensure that they do not have to. The post Seberang Jaya Hospital Leads The Way In The School In Hospital Initiative appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: 42-Hour Work Week For Nurses To Remain For Now: Dzulkefly
items.summary: Health Minister Dzulkefly Ahmad says nurses will continue working a 42-hour week for now, with a reduction to 38 hours only when staffing improves. MOH is offering incentives, career progression, and digitalisation to improve working conditions and retain nurses. The post 42-Hour Work Week For Nurses To Remain For Now: Dzulkefly appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Umno's doors open to all wanting to return, including Hamzah, says Zambry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Umno's doors remain open to anyone wishing to return to the party, including Opposition leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, following former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin's move to seek re-entry, says Supreme Council member Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir.
